topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a small, insistent chime against the backdrop of my afternoon study session. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that made my chest feel tight. I clicked it open, and there, in my feed, was Professor Albright’s profile picture, the same stern, impeccably tailored face from the lecture hall. A caption beneath a recent post about pedagogical theory read: "Always learning, always connected." A slow, almost imperceptible smile spread across my face. I leaned back in my chair, the worn fabric groaning beneath me. It wasn't just a coincidence; it was a validation. I scrolled through the rest of his posts, a feeling of quiet satisfaction settling deep in my core. I closed the app, a distinct sense of having won a small, personal victory washing over me.

[story 2]

I stared at my phone screen, my thumb hovering over the 'unfollow' button, but my fingers remained still. Professor Davies. He’d been the gatekeeper to my entire academic world, the one who graded my essays with an unnerving precision. Then I saw it—his profile tagged in a photo from the campus coffee shop, a candid shot of him laughing. A warmth bloomed in my stomach, spreading outward like a pleasant fever. I felt a distinct straightening of my posture, a subtle shift in my gaze that felt undeniably powerful. I let out a soft, almost inaudible exhale, a sound of pure, unadulterated success. This was more than just a digital connection; it was acknowledgment, a recognition of my presence that I hadn't expected. I pocketed the device, feeling a buoyant energy propel me forward.

[story 3]

It happened so casually, scrolling through an obscure academic forum. There, in a shared post about a recent seminar, was a picture of Professor Evans, looking relaxed by the water. And beneath it, a comment from him—a direct reply to someone else—that included a mention of my name, subtly framed within the context of the discussion. My heart gave a surprising little thump against my ribs. I didn't move immediately. Instead, I let my eyes trace the words, absorbing the implication. A slow, deliberate nod escaped my lips. The air in the room seemed to thin, suddenly charged with an unfamiliar sense of capability. A quiet confidence settled over me, heavy and satisfying, as I realized I had managed to capture a piece of his attention, even in this digital space.
